basics.name: Paulo V. Galvao Simplicio
basics.label: Ph.D. Candidate in Robotics Engineering
basics.summary: Ph.D. candidate in Robotics Engineering at West Virginia University, working on autonomous UAV systems for infrastructure inspection. Research spans motion planning, photogrammetry-aware coverage planning, real-time onboard replanning, and visual-inertial state estimation.

work.name: West Virginia University — Robotics Lab
work.summary: Lead UAV autonomy research on a DOE-funded program for autonomous inspection of coal-ash and tailings dams, owning the planning, perception-aware mapping, and onboard replanning stack.
highlights: Designed a multi-resolution motion planner (A*/RRT* on octrees, OMPL, Open3D) generating collision-free tunnel corridors for UAV inspection; published at ICUAS 2025.
Built a photogrammetry-aware coverage planner enforcing GSD, image-overlap, row-spacing, and view-geometry constraints; published at ICUAS 2024.
Engineered real-time online replanning with hazard detection and return-to-home (RTH) logic for battery-swap missions.
Training PPO policies in NVIDIA Isaac Lab for autonomous next-best-view (NBV) planning (ongoing).
Developing real-time state estimation fusing visual odometry with GNSS/IMU in factor graphs (GTSAM) (ongoing).

work.name: University of São Paulo — Center for Robotics (CROB)
highlights: Developed robust quadrotor controllers (PID, LQR, MPC, disturbance-observer-based) for trajectory tracking under wind gusts and parametric uncertainty; validated in Gazebo and on hardware.
Results published in IET Control Theory & Applications (2024) and IEEE Access (2022).

work.name: University of São Paulo
highlights: Led lab sections and office hours, graded projects, and refined lab materials and rubrics with the instructor.

work.name: Sennze Automation
highlights: Contributed to an automated wireline retrieval system with encoder-based depth tracking and safety alarms to prevent pull-head shoot-outs.
Advisor: Prof. G. A. S. Pereira
DOE-funded research on autonomous UAV inspection

awards.title: SARC–BARINet Aerospace Competition — International Winner
awards.summary: Flying U2 Team, University of São Paulo.

awards.title: Graduated Top of Class, B.Eng. Mechatronics Engineering

publications.name: Multi-Resolution UAV Path Replanning for Inspection of Tailings Dams
publications.summary: Multi-resolution motion planner (A*/RRT* on octrees, OMPL, Open3D) generating collision-free tunnel corridors for UAV inspection in cluttered environments.

publications.name: Mission Planning for Photogrammetry-Based Autonomous 3D Mapping of Dams Using a Commercial UAV
publications.summary: Photogrammetry-aware coverage planner enforcing GSD, image-overlap, row-spacing, and view-geometry constraints for high-fidelity 3D reconstruction.

publications.name: A Behavior Tree Approach for Battery-Aware Inspection of Large Structures Using Drones
publications.summary: Behavior tree framework for battery-aware drone inspection enabling autonomous battery-swap missions.

publications.name: Robust and Intelligent Control of Quadrotors Subject to Wind Gusts
publications.summary: Robust and intelligent quadrotor controllers (PID, LQR, MPC, disturbance-observer-based) for trajectory tracking under wind gusts and parametric uncertainty.

publications.name: Multi-UAV Collaborative System for the Identification of Surface Cyanobacterial Blooms and Aquatic Macrophytes
publications.summary: Multi-UAV system for autonomous identification of surface cyanobacterial blooms and aquatic macrophytes.

publications.name: Disturbance Observer-Based Robust Control of a Quadrotor Subject to Parametric Uncertainties and Wind Disturbance
publications.summary: Disturbance observer-based robust control of a quadrotor subject to parametric uncertainties and wind disturbance.

publications.name: Disturbance Observer-Based Control for Trajectory Tracking of a Quadrotor Subject to Uncertainties
publications.summary: Disturbance observer-based control for trajectory tracking of a quadrotor subject to uncertainties.

projects.name: XPRIZE Rainforest — Brazilian Team (Finalist)
projects.summary: Co-developed a ruggedized remotely-operated mobile robot for autonomous biodiversity sampling in tropical rainforests. Field campaigns in Brazil and Singapore.
highlights: Collaboration with biologists from Brazil and France

projects.name: SARC–BARINet Aerospace Competition — Flying U2 Team (International Winner)
projects.summary: Designed and tuned cascaded UAV controllers (PID, LQR, MPC) for autonomous flight over water under high-wind disturbance. Validated in Gazebo and field trials.
